explanation.`A: 2`, `B: 1`: Correct. Breaking at j == 2 prints heights 0 through 2 for each seal, and returning at i == 1 stops the program after the second seal (i = 0 and i = 1).
explanation.`A: 1`, `B: 2`: Incorrect. Breaking at j == 1 would stop printing at height 1 per seal, and waiting for i == 2 would incorrectly print a third seal.
explanation.`A: 2 - i`, `B: 1`: Incorrect. Making the inner break condition dependent on i causes Seal 1 to stop climbing prematurely at height 1 instead of height 2.
explanation.`A: 2`, `B: i + j == 3`: Incorrect. The variable j is out of scope in the outer loop, so this code would fail to compile.

explanation.`Stack<String> souvenirBag = new Stack<>: Correct. A Stack follows the LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle, ensuring the last item pushed is the first one popped — matching the required output order.
explanation.`List<String> souvenirBag = new ArrayLis: Incorrect. ArrayList uses add() and get() methods rather than push() and pop(), so the code would not compile as written.
explanation.`Set<String> souvenirBag = new HashSet<>: Incorrect. Set is an interface for unique unordered elements and does not support push() or pop() operations.
explanation.`Queue souvenirTube = new Queue();`: Incorrect. Queue follows FIFO logic, which would produce the wrong print order. Additionally, Queue is an interface and cannot be instantiated directly.

explanation.`System.out.print(mySeal.move() + \" \": Correct. Casting to VolcanoSeal gives access to describe(), which calls super.category() and returns "Generic-Mammal". Polymorphism ensures mySeal.move() calls the overridden version, returning "Climbs".
Incorrect. Due to polymorphism, mySeal.category() dispatches to the overridden version in VolcanoSeal and returns "Brave-Climber" instead of "Generic-Mammal".
explanation.`System.out.print(mySeal.category() + \": Incorrect. This reverses the output order and also incorrectly calls the overridden category(), returning "Brave-Climber" rather than "Generic-Mammal".
Incorrect. The super keyword is instance-specific and cannot be used inside a static main method to access parent class members.

learning_objectives: Apply nested loops with break and return to control program flow and predict the resulting output
Select the correct Java collection (Stack) based on LIFO behaviour required by push() and pop() operations
Predict method dispatch under polymorphism and use super and downcasting to access parent-class behaviour
